pred_reason: SWC-101 is applicable due to multiple reentrancy vulnerabilities in withdraw and fallback functions where external calls precede state changes. SWC-107 fits as inconsistent SafeMath usage and unprotected division create arithmetic risks. Evidence: withdraw function violates CEI pattern (lines ~198-204); SafeMath div lacks zero check (lines ~45-48); Direct assignments bypass overflow checks in multiple locations

findings.Description: The withdraw function makes external calls before state changes, violating checks-effects-interactions pattern. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential complete drainage of contract funds through reentrancy attacks

findings.Description: Fallback function performs external transfer before state updates, creating reentrancy vector. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Could disrupt token sale mechanics and allow state manipulation if gas costs change

findings.Description: SafeMath div function lacks zero-divisor check, causing revert on division by zero. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Denial-of-service conditions and unexpected contract behavior

findings.Description: setRaisedAmount function lacks owner restriction, allowing any user to modify fundraising state. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Token sale disruption and fundraising metric manipulation

findings.Description: transferFrom function checks frozen timestamp of msg.sender instead of _from, allowing bypass of frozen account restrictions. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Unauthorized token transfers from frozen accounts

findings.Issue: Missing frozen account checks in approve
findings.Description: approve function lacks frozen account validation, allowing frozen accounts to approve spenders. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Frozen accounts can indirectly transfer tokens through approved addresses
findings.Location: approve() function, missing frozen account checks

findings.Description: Contract inconsistently applies SafeMath protection, with direct assignments bypassing overflow checks. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential token supply manipulation and arithmetic overflow vulnerabilities
findings.Location: Multiple locations including totalSupply assignment and approve function

findings.Description: Owner has unlimited minting capability and uncontrolled fundraising parameter modification. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Token inflation, fundraising manipulation, and centralization risks

findings.Description: Fixed-rate token sale mechanism is susceptible to front-running attacks. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Unfair token distribution and potential market manipulation
